title: JSON Lines, CSV, HTML Reports, Accessibility, and Correction Workflows · Inference Brief: Correction Workflow

Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove JSONL and CSV contracts, accessible HTML, chart alternatives, corrections, and deterministic evidence; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Correction Workflow model · Theory Correction Wo…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Deleting a report row is a valid correction history.
